name: invariant_vault_amount_conservation
summary: Vault.amount stays equal to cumulative deposits minus cumulative withdrawals across all deposit and withdraw calls.
rationale: The ContractSurface exposes a single balance-bearing field, Vault.amount (u64), and exactly two movement-class instructions: deposit (adds `amount`) and withdraw (subtracts `amount`). The field name `amount` is a strong-signal name per the ranking rules. A fixture that tracks a running expected ledger (start + Σdeposits − Σwithdrawals) and compares it to the on-chain Vault.amount after every instruction provides tight conservation coverage with no ambiguity about which field to track.

name: invariant_withdraw_depositor_only
summary: withdraw rejects any signer that is not the vault's recorded depositor.
rationale: withdraw is the sole value-extracting instruction and its accounts list includes `depositor`, which in a single-owner vault pattern is the authority key stored at initialization. Any caller other than vault.depositor draining the vault represents the canonical unauthorized-withdrawal vulnerability. This invariant probes that boundary directly by sending a withdraw transaction signed by an attacker key and asserting the transaction fails with an access-control error.

name: invariant_deposit_depositor_signer
summary: deposit rejects callers who are not the vault's authorized depositor signer.
rationale: The deposit instruction surfaces `depositor` in its accounts alongside `vault`, suggesting the program binds the depositor key at initialize time. If the program enforces that only the recorded depositor may push funds in (preventing griefing or front-running with manipulated amounts), this invariant captures that gate. Ranked below the withdraw variant because many vault designs intentionally allow open deposits; the signal is present but weaker than for withdrawal.
